--- title: AA:X Packets layout: aa ---

AA:X Packet Hub

Packet index for the AA:X shared foundation

AA:X is the shared strand completed in whichever AA course comes first. Unlike AA:SS and AA:U, it is less naturally represented as a strict sequence. The topics are foundational and cross-connect with both courses, so a card layout makes more sense here than a numbered progression.

These pages are intended to become the long-term support layer for the shared tools of the program: geometry and units, dimensional reasoning, dynamics, waves, spectroscopy, and the larger physical framework in which those tools sit.

Packets

Geometry and Units

Triangles, radians, parsecs, astronomical units, and the geometric/measurement vocabulary used throughout AA.

Dimensional Analysis

Significant figures, units, physical dimensions, constants, scaling, estimation, and order-of-magnitude reasoning.

Astrodynamics

Newton’s laws, gravitation, circular motion, energy, center of mass, escape speed, and Kepler-style combinations.

Waves and Light

Oscillation, wave propagation, optics, diffraction, resolution, the spectrum, and Doppler shift.

Spectroscopy

The photoelectric effect, Bohr’s atom, hydrogen, spectral lines, line broadening, and how light reveals physics.

Thermal and Modern Physics

Thermodynamics, statistical ideas, relativity, quantum foundations, and nuclear/particle concepts that support later AA work. :contentReference[oaicite:25]{index=25}

How to use this hub

AA:X is where many of the shared conceptual tools of the program live. Later, each packet page can grow into a page with notes, reminders, quick derivations, and links to the AA:SS or AA:U packets that depend on it.

Quick jump

  • Geometry and Units
  • Dimensional Analysis
  • Astrodynamics
  • Waves and Light
  • Spectroscopy
  • Thermal and Modern Physics
